skill.name: git-pr-workflows-git-workflow
skill.description: Guide AI agents through a complete git workflow, from review and testing to Conventional Commits, branch preparation, and pull request creation.
skill.summary: Coordinate code review, testing, commits, branch checks, and pull request preparation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown workflow instructions, CLI flag names, ordinary words containing algorithm substrings, and one legitimate git rollback example. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, hidden scripts, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Static command detections are Markdown guidance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain a resource reference, supported CLI flag names, and a branch naming pattern. They are not executable code and do not create a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a Markdown instructions file with option names and text examples. No script file, shell interpolation, or automated execution block is present.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography detections are substring false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak algorithm findings occur in normal prose, including words such as description and designated. The skill contains no cryptographic implementation or recommendation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and headings, not hash functions, cipher calls, or security configuration. The static pattern matched common English substrings.

low_findings.title: Rollback procedure references git revert
low_findings.description: The skill suggests creating a revert pull request with git revert during rollback. This is a normal git workflow command, but agents should still ask before changing repository history.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command reference is explicit and legitimate for the skill domain. Risk is low because it appears in a rollback checklist, not an executable script.

low_findings.title: Network reconnaissance detection is a false positive
low_findings.description: The network finding points to the --draft-pr flag documentation. No scanning tool, host enumeration, network request, or reconnaissance instruction appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a supported option for creating a draft pull request. There is no semantic network behavior attached to the text.

content.user_title: Run a Complete Git PR Workflow
content.value_statement: Teams lose time when code review, testing, commits, and pull request setup happen in separate ad hoc steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for review-ready git changes.
actual_capabilities: Guides pre-commit code quality review using specialized reviewer agents.
Coordinates test execution, coverage review, and missing test recommendations.
Creates Conventional Commit message guidance based on categorized changes.
Checks branch strategy for feature branch or trunk-based development.
Builds pull request descriptions, metadata, reviewer guidance, and merge strategy recommendations.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an executable git automation script.
It depends on available subagents matching the names used in the instructions.
It cannot guarantee CI, branch protection, or pull request creation without tool access.
It may need adaptation for teams with nonstandard branching or commit policies.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Pull Request
use_cases.description: Review local changes, run tests, create a Conventional Commit, and prepare a complete PR description.

use_cases.title: Standardize Release Branch Checks
use_cases.description: Apply consistent validation steps before pushing a release branch or opening a production-bound PR.

use_cases.title: Coach Contributors on Git Process
use_cases.description: Give contributors a repeatable checklist for reviews, tests, commit structure, and rollback planning.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for local uncommitted changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this git workflow skill to review my current changes, identify risks, and tell me what must be fixed before a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard feature branch pull request preparation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full feature branch workflow against main. Include review findings, test status, a Conventional Commit message, and a pull request description.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Trunk-Based Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for teams that merge small changes to main.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the trunk-based workflow. Confirm branch readiness, test coverage, rollback considerations, and any feature flag requirements before push.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Safe Rollback
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced incident response after a problematic merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rollback section to plan a revert PR, communication steps, validation checks, and postmortem notes for the last merged change.

output_examples.input: Prepare my feature branch for a pull request.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow report with review findings, test results, commit message recommendation, branch readiness, and PR description sections.

output_examples.input: Check if this change is safe for trunk-based development.
output_examples.output: A readiness summary covering conflicts, CI expectations, feature flags, branch protection, and blocking issues.

output_examples.input: Create a rollback plan for a failed deployment.
output_examples.output: A rollback checklist with revert PR guidance, feature flag actions, team communication, validation steps, and root cause analysis notes.

best_practices: Run review and test phases before generating commits or pull request metadata.
Keep commits atomic and use Conventional Commits when the repository expects them.
Confirm branch protection, sensitive data checks, and rollback steps before pushing.
anti_patterns: Skipping tests because the workflow can still generate a pull request description.
Using the same branch strategy for every repository without checking team policy.
Letting agents push, revert, or change history without explicit user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill run git commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow instructions and command guidance. Tool execution depends on the host assistant and user approval.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests in GitHub?
faq.answer: It can prepare PR descriptions and metadata guidance. Actual PR creation requires a connected git or GitHub tool.

faq.question: Does it support Conventional Commits?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a phase for categorizing changes and drafting Conventional Commit messages.

faq.question: Can I skip automated tests?
faq.answer: The skill documents a skip-tests option, but it recommends caution because test results inform later workflow steps.

faq.question: Is it only for feature branches?
faq.answer: No. It includes guidance for both feature branch workflows and trunk-based development.

faq.question: What tools does it need?
faq.answer: It works best with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code plus available review, testing, documentation, and deployment agents.
